selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 7.8% and 20-day momentum is 17.0%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 11.7% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 3.5%, downside volatility near 3.0%, and a recent max drawdown of 9.6%.
Volume confirmation is 2.8% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 7.2% versus SPY over 5 days and 7.2% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.91, confidence 0.88, and 1.4 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.91, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 2 sources, 1.3 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.92, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.88.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.01).
Overall model score is 0.267 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Bank of America Maintains Buy Rating on Meta Platforms (META) Stock
news_evidence.llm_reason: Analyst (BofA) reiterated a Buy and $835 target — company-specific but a routine note with little new data. May modestly influence sentiment/flow in the short term but lacks fresh catalysts or financials.

news_evidence.title: Should Nebius and CoreWeave Investors Be Scared by Meta's Latest Plans?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Discusses Meta's apparent plans to lease spare cloud capacity — a direct company action that could meaningfully affect near-term revenue monetization and competitive dynamics with cloud-focused partners. Impact is potentially positive but timing/scale are unclear, so short-term effect is uncertain.
